name: Biome Generation
description: A generation process that assigns biome identities, boundaries, resources, hazards, and visual rules across a map or world.
design_notes: Biome generation gives procedural space identity. Useful biome systems track biome_id, noise masks, climate inputs, adjacency rules, transition widths, content_version, and validation so the world reads as varied without producing impossible resource or traversal combinations.
design_purpose: Create recognizable regions with distinct visuals, resources, enemies, and traversal constraints.
Control pacing by placing safer, harsher, richer, or more specialized zones in meaningful patterns.
Support replayable worlds while keeping resource access and navigation solvable.
player_fantasy: The player feels they are crossing distinct regions with different moods, risks, and opportunities.

parameters.name: biome_id
parameters.description: Canonical biome rule set applied to terrain, decoration, encounters, and resources.

parameters.name: biome_noise_scale
parameters.description: Noise sampling scale used to define biome regions or masks.

parameters.name: transition_width_tiles
parameters.description: Blend width between adjacent biome masks or material zones.

parameters.name: adjacency_rule_id
parameters.description: Rule that controls which biome pairs can touch or blend.

parameters.name: resource_density_multiplier
parameters.description: Biome-specific modifier for resource, loot, or encounter placement density.

edge_cases: A required resource appears only in a biome that the seed failed to place near reachable progression routes.
Biome transitions create narrow hazard strips, visual seams, or mismatched collision/navigation rules.
Adjacent biome rules reject every candidate and require fallback_template_id or repair passes.
Content patches change biome_id rules and old saves need compatibility for discovered map regions.
common_bugs: Biome masks use different coordinates than terrain chunks, causing decoration and ground textures to disagree.
Resource placement samples biome_id before transition blending and spawns desert resources in forest visuals.
RNG stream sharing means adding a new biome changes loot or encounter generation elsewhere.
Debug maps show colors but not adjacency_rule_id or rejected placement reasons.
balancing_notes: Biome size should support travel goals: tiny regions feel noisy, while huge regions can starve players of needed resources.
Resource density and enemy difficulty need to be tuned per biome so visual variety also affects play.
Rare biomes are exciting only if players have clues, maps, or systems that make finding them reasonable.
accessibility_notes: Use shapes, landmarks, labels, and material changes in addition to color to distinguish biome boundaries.
Avoid dense procedural decoration that hides hazards, enemies, or interactables without visibility options.

implementation_notes.unity: Generate biome masks from chunk_coordinate and generation_seed, then pass biome_id into terrain, foliage, resource, and encounter systems; draw editor overlays for masks and adjacency failures.
implementation_notes.godot: Keep biome rules in Resources or JSON and sample them before TileMap or mesh decoration; save content_version and discovered biome IDs for map compatibility.
implementation_notes.unreal: Use PCG graphs or custom runtime systems with biome_id metadata; validate biome adjacency, navigation, and spawn/resource rules before streaming a region active.
implementation_notes.web: Use deterministic noise and integer chunk coordinates to assign biome_id; export biome masks in debug views so bad seeds can be reproduced from seed and content_version.

example_games.title: Minecraft
example_games.note: Uses generated biomes with distinct terrain and resources.

example_games.title: Terraria
example_games.note: Uses biome regions with specific enemies and materials.
